title: Slow-cooker bolognese sauce
subtitle: Low and slow ragu with beef mince and red wine
excerpt: Beef mince slow-cooked with onion, carrot, celery, red wine, and tinned tomatoes for six to eight hours. The long cook melds the soffritto into the sauce in a way that twenty minutes on the stove cannot. Use for pasta, lasagne, or jacket potatoes.
sourceNotes: Bolognese is a Bolognese meat sauce that became one of the most cooked pasta dishes in British home kitchens from the 1970s onwards. The slow cooker version became standard because the long, low heat suits ground meat dishes — it breaks down the proteins slowly and melds the vegetables into the sauce rather than leaving them as distinct pieces. The British version typically uses beef mince, tinned tomatoes, and red wine rather than the traditional milk-and-white-wine Bolognese of Emilia-Romagna.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes extremely well for up to 3 months. Defrost in the fridge overnight.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe — the slow cooker handles the extra volume and bolognese freezes perfectly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. The sauce thickens and the flavours deepen as it rests.

content.text: Brown the mince before it goes in. A slow cooker is wet heat from start to finish — mince dropped in raw will cook to grey pellets rather than breaking into the sauce. Three minutes in a very hot pan, broken up thoroughly, changes the final texture completely.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over high heat. Fry the {{onion}}, {{carrot}}, and {{celery}} for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}} and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the {{beef-mince}} and break it up thoroughly, cooking until browned all over with no pink remaining. Pour in the {{red-wine}} and let it bubble for 1 minute. Tip everything into the slow cooker. Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{stock-beef}}, {{bay-leaves}}, {{oregano-dried}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Stir to combine. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours. Remove the {{bay-leaves}}, taste and adjust seasoning. The sauce should be thick and deeply coloured.

items.cause: Too much liquid or lid not sealing properly
items.fix: Remove the lid and cook on high for the last 30 minutes to reduce. For a thicker result from the start, omit the stock entirely

items.symptom: Mince in clumps rather than broken into the sauce
items.cause: Not broken up thoroughly before slow cooking
items.fix: The mince must be broken into small pieces in the pan before adding to the slow cooker — large clumps will not break down further in wet heat

items.symptom: Sauce tastes flat despite the long cook
items.cause: Seasoning not adjusted at the end
items.fix: Always taste and season after the cook. The long reduction concentrates salt — taste before adding more. A small splash of red wine vinegar can also lift a flat sauce

content.text: Slow-cooker bolognese is a batch-cook staple. Start it in the morning and have pasta ready for dinner with no further effort. It freezes in portions and tastes better reheated the next day. Use any leftovers for lasagne — the sauce is already the right consistency to layer.
